name: Stir-Fry Ostrich and Tomato
recipeIngredient: 1 pound ostrich steak, skinless, thinly sliced on the diagonal across the grain
¼ teaspoon white pepper, freshly ground
2–3 tablespoons peanut oil or corn oil
½ teaspoon red pepper flakes, or more, optional
½ yellow onion, cut into eighths and layers separated
1 red or green bell pepper, cut into ½" square pieces
½ teaspoon sesame oil, or less for flavor
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, toss together the meat, soy sauce, dry sherry, salt, and white pepper; set aside.
2. Heat a large wok or heavy skillet over medium-high to high heat.
3. Add peanut oil (or corn oil) and heat until the oil sizzles.
4. Add ginger root, garlic, and red pepper flakes. Stir-fry for 1 minute.
5. Add the reserved meat mixture, separating the slices, and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes. Do not overcook.
6. Remove the meat with a slotted spoon and set aside.
7. Add more oil as needed, then stir-fry onion and bell pepper until they begin to soften (about 2 minutes).
8. Add tomatoes and cover; heat until steam rises (about 5 minutes).
9. Return the meat to the wok, add sesame oil, and stir briefly to heat through.
10. Serve immediately and garnish with cilantro sprigs.
